mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.3pp UNRATE increase over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions relative to its moving average show zero predictive power for 20-day returns during high-inflation periods with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut exceeding 25 basis points in a single meeting signals liquidity injection that immediately boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive following >25bp rate cuts with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that forces wage inflation, eroding corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence shows no negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample with Rank IC above -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit zero predictive correlation with 20-day SPX returns across all business cycle phases with R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, any positive month-over-month change in industrial production signals capacity utilization rebound that immediately lifts equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO positive changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery periods with accuracy below 50%.
